steps.title: Spot the contrast signal
steps.content:
The word However at the beginning of the second sentence signals a contrast between the claim about the word’s first use and the actual language Chaucer wrote in.
This contrast tells us that the blank after the semicolon needs a word that introduces a correction or alternative.

steps.title: Apply the contrast to the choices
steps.content:
The transition rather expresses a correction—instead—which fits the contrast set up by However.
The other three choices either show cause‑effect (as a result,), similarity (similarly,) or sequence (finally,) and do not match the contrast.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
